name: adf-master
description: Complete Azure Data Factory expertise system with comprehensive JSON references, validation enforcement, Microsoft Fabric integration, and ML/analytics orchestration (2025-2026). PROACTIVELY activate for: (1) ANY ADF pipeline JSON creation or editing, (2) Activity configuration (Copy, ForEach, IfCondition, Switch, Until, Lookup, ExecutePipeline, WebActivity, DatabricksJob, AzureMLExecutePipeline), (3) Activity nesting validation (ForEach/If/Switch/Until limitations with Execute Pipeline workarounds), (4) Linked service JSON (Blob Storage accountKind, Azure SQL, ADLS Gen2, Synapse, Databricks, Fabric Lakehouse/Warehouse, Azure ML, REST, SFTP, Snowflake), (5) Expression functions (@utcnow, @formatDateTime, @concat, @activity(), @pipeline().parameters), (6) Trigger configuration (Schedule, Tumbling Window with dependencies, Event triggers), (7) Dataset JSON schemas with parameterization, (8) CI/CD setup (GitHub Actions/Azure DevOps with Node.js 20.x, @microsoft/azure-data-factory-utilities), (9) ARM template generation and multi-environment deployment, (10) Microsoft Fabric integration (ADF mounting, Invoke Pipeline cross-workspace, Variable Libraries), (11) Machine learning orchestration (Azure ML pipelines, batch endpoints, Azure AI Services, Databricks ML/MLflow, feature engineering with Data Flows), (12) Azure SQL to Storage archival patterns for analysis, (13) Pipeline debugging and error troubleshooting. Provides: Complete JSON reference library for all activity types, linked services, triggers, datasets, and expressions. ML pipeline patterns for training, scoring, and data archival. Validation rules with resource limits (80/120 activities, 50 parameters, 50 batchCount). Progressive disclosure references for deep technical details. Commands for validation, debugging, and generation. Utility scripts for ARM parameters, dependency graphs, and environment comparison.
